title: campanula thyrsoides subsp. thyrsoides
intro: Campanula thyrsoides subsp. thyrsoides, also known as the Eastern Bellflower, is a perennial plant species that belongs to the Campanulaceae family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties that help relieve congestion. The plant's leaves and flowers have been used to create herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which also offer antioxidant and anti-inflammatory benefits, promoting overall health and well-being. Additionally, the plant is said to have antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and skin issues.
